microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's **enterprise creator equivalent** — a recovery validation program — from a 2-person operation into a platformized system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual drills and drive **$14M+ impact** across Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Built an **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, restructuring execution cycles to cut planning time by 39% and increase drill capacity by 21% — translating one-off partner needs into durable platform capabilities.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing capability, delivering the program in **4 months** with a 94% recovery rate by synthesizing bespoke partner requests into reusable primitives adopted org-wide.

Prioritized a cross-functional backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets**, facilitating sprint planning and retrospectives that stabilized the developer intake process and created clarity and accountability across engineering and ops teams.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first GTM strategy — optimizing the website and Google profile to strengthen direct audience connection and organic discovery.

tailoring_notes: 'Reusable primitives / durable platform capabilities' (JD term) woven into microsoft_ft bullet 2, microsoft_2022 bullet 1, and amazon_robotics bullet 3 to mirror Substack's core enterprise PM mandate.
'Synthesizing partner/creator needs into scalable systems' (JD framing) used in microsoft_ft bullets 1 and 5, and microsoft_2022 bullet 1 to directly echo the role's stated purpose.
'Go-to-market strategy' (JD requirement) surfaced explicitly in pro_painters bullet 1 and bullet 2, positioning Cyrus as someone who has owned GTM end-to-end.
'User research / creator empathy' (JD requirement) called out in microsoft_2023 bullet 2 with the 11+ interview detail, and microsoft_2022 bullet 2 with 20+ service team discovery sessions.
